title: Honcho Memory
summary: Honcho is an AI-native memory provider plugin that adds dialectic reasoning and deep user modeling to Hermes, maintaining a running model of user preferences, communication style, and goals through LLM-synthesized reasoning across sessions.

cli_commands.purpose: Connection status, config, and key settings
Show or update peer names and dialectic reasoning level
Seed or show the AI peer's Honcho identity
Sync Honcho config to all existing profiles
Map current directory to a Honcho session name
Enable Honcho for the active profile
Disable Honcho for the active profile

config_keys.description: Set to 'honcho' to activate Honcho as the memory provider
Token budget for auto-injected context per turn (in honcho.json)
Turns between peer.chat() LLM calls (dialectic refresh)
Number of .chat() passes per dialectic invocation (1-3)

code_examples.caption: Interactive setup
code_examples.code: hermes memory setup    # select "honcho" from the provider list

gotchas: hermes honcho subcommand only appears after memory.provider: honcho is set in config.yaml
dialecticCadence recommended 1-5; too frequent increases LLM call costs
In tools mode, dialecticCadence and contextCadence are irrelevant — model calls tools explicitly
Server-side observation toggles (from Honcho dashboard) override local config
Session prewarm fires at session init — if it hasn't landed by turn 1, falls back to synchronous call with bounded timeout
full_text:
# Honcho Memory

Honcho is an AI-native memory backend that adds dialectic reasoning and deep user modeling on top of Hermes's built-in memory system.
